description: A designed poster built on a vintage early-1900s photochrom/colorized photograph of the Statue of Liberty in New York Harbor, with soft teal-green water and sky and the warm-toned statue and star-fort base. At top is a small white White House line-engraving, beneath which large white serif capitals read 'WORTH PROTECTING'. A small white caption at lower-right reads 'Statue of Liberty, New-York Harbor, 1905'. The aesthetic is nostalgic and dignified (antique image, refined serif type). The messaging technique pairs a classic symbol of American liberty/immigration with the slogan 'WORTH PROTECTING', reframing the icon to support the administration's border/immigration-enforcement and nationalist messaging ('a beacon of freedom, hope & the American spirit'). Target audience: the general public, evoking patriotic heritage to justify protective/enforcement policy.
notable_text: WORTH PROTECTING / Statue of Liberty, New-York Harbor, 1905
